You are working on the Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime repository.
The codebase is at version 0.6 (commit: e721269c).

## Issue to Resolve

API reference improvements
It would be nice to systematically and extensively improve our API reference:

For every API endpoint, it would be nice to provide the following (example is the place where the property does not hold):

* Extensively document what the entity represents, whether it follows ISO-8601, **how the entity can be acquired**, and what time scale it comes from. [Example](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx.datetime/-day-of-week/) 
* Provide a minimum of how the entity can be used. [Example](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx.datetime/-fixed-offset-time-zone/)
* Thoroughly explain the allowed and expected contract and behaviours and system-specifics. [Example](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx.datetime/-clock/)
* If applicable, provide contexts in which the entity is expected/recommended to be used (e.g. our `README` shines at that) and how it interacts with other (core) entities. [Example](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx.datetime/-local-date-time/)
* Provide a basic usage example. [Example](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx.datetime/-time-zone/)
* If applicable, provide details about conversion rules, gotchas and recommendations. [Example](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx.datetime/-instant/)
* Where appropriate, information about conversions and interactions with native types (e.g. Instant <-> Java Instant conversion)
* Ensure that information used in our `README` is, in fact, deducible from the core entities documentation

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

> If applicable, provide details about conversion rules, gotchas and recommendations. [Example](https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx-datetime/kotlinx.datetime/-instant/)

I don't know of gotchas related to `Instant`. The only one that comes to mind is that `instant + 1.days` is a non-calendar operation (this could be worth documenting in the `plus` function), but I'd argue it's `Duration`'s gotcha. `Instant` seems tough to use incorrectly to me. What gotchas did you have in mind?

> Where appropriate, information about conversions and interactions with native types (e.g. Instant <-> Java Instant conversion)

When you have specific behavior on some platforms, you document it in the common code, makes sense; but when you have some extra interactions with platform code, you can't document them in common code (because symbols aren't resolved). If you have platform-specific documentation, in the IDE, it is either ignored (when writing common code) or replaces the common documentation. In the latter case, you have to repeat the common documentation for platforms *and* include platform-specific additions, which I think just isn't worth it.

If the system was a bit different and platform-specific documentation was *added* to the common documentation (and maybe the IDE would be smart enough to show the documentation for the platforms the project actually uses), then I agree that mentioning platform-specific converters and so on would be beneficial. Or better yet, if there was "extension documentation" along with extension functions: then we'd be able to show `NSDate` conversions in the `Instant` documentation for Darwin targets.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `README.md`
- `core/build.gradle.kts`
- `core/common/src/Clock.kt`
- `core/common/src/DateTimePeriod.kt`
- `core/common/src/DateTimeUnit.kt`
- `core/common/src/DayOfWeek.kt`
- `core/common/src/Exceptions.kt`
- `core/common/src/Instant.kt`
- `core/common/src/LocalDate.kt`
- `core/common/src/LocalDateTime.kt`
- `core/common/src/LocalTime.kt`
- `core/common/src/Month.kt`
- `core/common/src/TimeZone.kt`
- `core/common/src/UtcOffset.kt`
- `core/common/src/format/DateTimeComponents.kt`
- `core/common/src/format/DateTimeFormat.kt`
- `core/common/src/format/DateTimeFormatBuilder.kt`
- `core/common/src/format/LocalDateFormat.kt`
- `core/common/src/format/Unicode.kt`
- `core/common/src/serializers/DateTimePeriodSerializers.kt`
- `core/common/src/serializers/InstantSerializers.kt`
- `core/common/src/serializers/LocalDateSerializers.kt`
- `core/common/src/serializers/LocalDateTimeSerializers.kt`
- `core/common/src/serializers/LocalTimeSerializers.kt`
- `core/common/src/serializers/TimeZoneSerializers.kt`
- `core/commonJs/src/TimeZone.kt`
- `core/commonJs/src/UtcOffset.kt`
- `core/jvm/src/TimeZoneJvm.kt`
- `core/jvm/src/UtcOffsetJvm.kt`
- `core/native/src/TimeZone.kt`
- `core/native/src/UtcOffset.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 6→package kotlinx.datetime
 7→
 8→/**
 9→ * The enumeration class representing the days of the week.
10→ */
11→public expect enum class DayOfWeek {
12→    MONDAY,
13→    TUESDAY,
14→    WEDNESDAY,
15→    THURSDAY,
16→    FRIDAY,
17→    SATURDAY,
18→    SUNDAY;
19→}
20→
21→/**
22→ * The ISO-8601 number of the given day of the week. Monday is 1, Sunday is 7.
23→ */
24→public val DayOfWeek.isoDayNumber: Int get() = ordinal + 1
25→
26→/**
27→ * Returns the [DayOfWeek] instance for the given ISO-8601 week day number. Monday is 1, Sunday is 7.
28→ */
29→public fun DayOfWeek(isoDayNumber: Int): DayOfWeek {
30→    require(isoDayNumber in 1..7) { "Expected ISO day-of-week number in 1..7, got $isoDayNumber" }
31→    return DayOfWeek.entries[isoDayNumber - 1]
32→}

1→/*
  2→ * Copyright 2019-2020 JetBrains s.r.o.
  3→ * Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 License that can be found in the LICENSE.txt file.
  4→ */
  5→
  6→package kotlinx.datetime
  7→
  8→import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.*
 10→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.InstantIso8601Serializer
 11→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 12→import kotlin.time.*
 13→
 14→/**
 15→ * A moment in time.
 16→ *
 17→ * A point in time must be uniquely identified, so that it is independent of a time zone.
 18→ * For example, `1970-01-01, 00:00:00` does not represent a moment in time, since this would happen at different times
 19→ * in different time zones: someone in Tokyo would think its already `1970-01-01` several hours earlier than someone in
 20→ * Berlin would. To represent such entities, use [LocalDateTime].
 21→ * In contrast, "the moment the clocks in London first showed 00:00 on Jan 1, 2000" is a specific moment
 22→ * in time, as is "1970-01-01, 00:00:00 UTC+0", and so it can be represented as an [Instant].
 23→ *
 24→ * `Instant` uses the UTC-SLS (smeared leap second) time scale. This time scale doesn't contain instants
 25→ * corresponding to leap seconds, but instead "smears" positive and negative leap seconds among the last 1000 seconds
 26→ * of the day when a leap second happens.
 27→ *
 28→ * Some ways in which [Instant] can be acquired are:
 29→ * - [Clock.now] can be used to query the current moment for the given clock. With [Clock.System], it is the current
 30→ *   moment as the platform sees it.
 31→ * - [Instant.parse] parses an ISO-8601 string.
 32→ * - [Instant.fromEpochMilliseconds] and [Instant.fromEpochSeconds] construct the instant values from the amount of time
 33→ *   since `1970-01-01T00:00:00Z` (the Unix epoch).
 34→ */
 35→@Serializable(with = InstantIso8601Serializer::class)
 36→public expect class Instant : Comparable<Instant> {
 37→
 38→    /**
 39→     * The number of seconds from the epoch instant `1970-01-01T00:00:00Z` rounded down to a [Long] number.
 40→     *
 41→     * The difference between the rounded number of seconds and the actual number of seconds
 42→     * is returned by [nanosecondsOfSecond] property expressed in nanoseconds.
 43→     *
 44→     * Note that this number doesn't include leap seconds added or removed since the epoch.
 45→     *
 46→     * @see Instant.fromEpochSeconds
 47→     */
 48→    public val epochSeconds: Long
 49→
 50→    /**
 51→     * The number of nanoseconds by which this instant is later than [epochSeconds] from the epoch instant.
 52→     *
 53→     * The value is always positive and lies in the range `0..999_999_999`.
 54→     *
 55→     * @see Instant.fromEpochSeconds
 56→     */
 57→    public val nanosecondsOfSecond: Int
 58→
 59→    /**
 60→     * Returns the number of milliseconds from the epoch instant `1970-01-01T00:00:00Z`.
 61→     *
 62→     * Any fractional part of millisecond is rounded down to the whole number of milliseconds.
 63→     *
 64→     * If the result does not fit in [Long], returns [Long.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Long.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
 65→     *
 66→     * @see Instant.fromEpochMilliseconds
 67→     */
 68→    public fun toEpochMilliseconds(): Long
 69→
 70→    /**
 71→     * Returns an instant that is the result of adding the specified [duration] to this instant.
 72→     *
 73→     * If the [duration] is positive, the returned instant is later than this instant.
 74→     * If the [duration] is negative, the returned instant is earlier than this instant.
 75→     *
 76→     * The return value is clamped to the platform-specific boundaries for [Instant] if the result exceeds them.
 77→     */
 78→    public operator fun plus(duration: Duration): Instant
 79→
 80→    /**
 81→     * Returns an instant that is the result of subtracting the specified [duration] from this instant.
 82→     *
 83→     * If the [duration] is positive, the returned instant is earlier than this instant.
 84→     * If the [duration] is negative, the returned instant is later than this instant.
 85→     *
 86→     * The return value is clamped to the platform-specific boundaries for [Instant] if the result exceeds them.
 87→     */
 88→    public operator fun minus(duration: Duration): Instant
 89→
 90→    // questionable
 91→    /**
 92→     * Returns the [Duration] between two instants: [other] and `this`.
 93→     *
 94→     * The duration returned is positive if this instant is later than the other,
 95→     * and negative if this instant is earlier than the other.
 96→     *
 97→     * The result is never clamped, but note that for instants that are far apart,
 98→     * the value returned may represent the duration between them inexactly due to the loss of precision.
 99→     */
100→    public operator fun minus(other: Instant): Duration
101→
102→    /**
103→     * Compares `this` instant with the [other] instant.
104→     * Returns zero if this instant represents the same moment as the other (i.e. equal to other),
105→     * a negative number if this instant is earlier than the other,
106→     * and a positive number if this instant is later than the other.
107→     */
108→    public override operator fun compareTo(other: Instant): Int
109→
110→    /**
111→     * Converts this instant to the ISO-8601 string representation.
112→     *
113→     * The representation uses the UTC-SLS time scale, instead of UTC.
114→     * In practice, this means that leap second handling will not be readjusted to the UTC.
115→     * Leap seconds will not be added or skipped, so it is impossible to acquire a string
116→     * where the component for seconds is 60, and for any day, it's possible to observe 23:59:59.
117→     *
118→     * @see Instant.parse
119→     * @see DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET for a very similar format. The difference is that
120→     * [DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET] will not add trailing zeros for readability to the
121→     * fractional part of the second.
122→     */
123→    public override fun toString(): String
124→
125→
126→    public companion object {
127→        @Deprecated("Use Clock.System.now() instead", ReplaceWith("Clock.System.now()", "kotlinx.datetime.Clock"), level = DeprecationLevel.ERROR)
128→        public fun now(): Instant
129→
130→        /**
131→         * Returns an [Instant] that is [epochMilliseconds] number of milliseconds from the epoch instant `1970-01-01T00:00:00Z`.
132→         *
133→         * The return value is clamped to the platform-specific boundaries for [Instant] if the result exceeds them.
134→         *
135→         * @see Instant.toEpochMilliseconds
136→         */
137→        public fun fromEpochMilliseconds(epochMilliseconds: Long): Instant
138→
139→        /**
140→         * Returns an [Instant] that is the [epochSeconds] number of seconds from the epoch instant `1970-01-01T00:00:00Z`
141→         * and the [nanosecondAdjustment] number of nanoseconds from the whole second.
142→         *
143→         * The return value is clamped to the platform-specific boundaries for [Instant] if the result exceeds them.
144→         */
145→        public fun fromEpochSeconds(epochSeconds: Long, nanosecondAdjustment: Long = 0): Instant
146→
147→        /**
148→         * Returns an [Instant] that is the [epochSeconds] number of seconds from the epoch instant `1970-01-01T00:00:00Z`
149→         * and the [nanosecondAdjustment] number of nanoseconds from the whole second.
150→         *
151→         * The return value is clamped to the platform-specific boundaries for [Instant] if the result exceeds them.
152→         */
153→        public fun fromEpochSeconds(epochSeconds: Long, nanosecondAdjustment: Int): Instant
154→
155→        /**
156→         * A shortcut for calling [DateTimeFormat.parse], followed by [DateTimeComponents.toInstantUsingOffset].
157→         *
158→         * Parses a string that represents an instant including date and time components and a mandatory
159→         * time zone offset and returns the parsed [Instant] value.
160→         *
161→         * The string is considered to represent time on the UTC-SLS time scale instead of UTC.
162→         * In practice, this means that, even if there is a leap second on the given day, it will not affect how the
163→         * time is parsed, even if it's in the last 1000 seconds of the day.
164→         * Instead, even if there is a negative leap second on the given day, 23:59:59 is still considered valid time.
165→         * 23:59:60 is invalid on UTC-SLS, so parsing it will fail.
166→         *
167→         * If the format is not specified, [DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET] is used.
168→         *
169→         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text cannot be parsed or the boundaries of [Instant] are exceeded.
170→         *
171→         * @see Instant.toString for formatting using the default format.
172→         * @see Instant.format for formatting using a custom format.
173→         */
174→        public fun parse(
175→            input: CharSequence,
176→            format: DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents> = DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET
177→        ): Instant
178→
179→        /**
180→         * An instant value that is far in the past.
181→         *
182→         * All instants in the range `DISTANT_PAST..DISTANT_FUTURE` can be [converted][Instant.toLocalDateTime] to
183→         * [LocalDateTime] without exceptions on all supported platforms.
184→         */
185→        public val DISTANT_PAST: Instant // -100001-12-31T23:59:59.999999999Z
186→
187→        /**
188→         * An instant value that is far in the future.
189→         *
190→         * All instants in the range `DISTANT_PAST..DISTANT_FUTURE` can be [converted][Instant.toLocalDateTime] to
191→         * [LocalDateTime] without exceptions on all supported platforms.
192→         */
193→        public val DISTANT_FUTURE: Instant // +100000-01-01T00:00:00Z
194→
195→        internal val MIN: Instant
196→        internal val MAX: Instant
197→    }
198→}
199→
200→/** Returns true if the instant is [Instant.DISTANT_PAST] or earlier. */
201→public val Instant.isDistantPast: Boolean
202→    get() = this <= Instant.DISTANT_PAST
203→
204→/** Returns true if the instant is [Instant.DISTANT_FUTURE] or later. */
205→public val Instant.isDistantFuture: Boolean
206→    get() = this >= Instant.DISTANT_FUTURE
207→
208→/**
209→ * @suppress
210→ */
211→@Deprecated("Removed to support more idiomatic code. See https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime/issues/339", ReplaceWith("Instant.parse(this)"), DeprecationLevel.WARNING)
212→public fun String.toInstant(): Instant = Instant.parse(this)
213→
214→/**
215→ * Returns an instant that is the result of adding components of [DateTimePeriod] to this instant. The components are
216→ * added in the order from the largest units to the smallest, i.e. from years to nanoseconds.
217→ *
218→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if this value or the results of intermediate computations are too large to fit in
219→ * [LocalDateTime].
220→ */
221→public expect fun Instant.plus(period: DateTimePeriod, timeZone: TimeZone): Instant
222→
223→/**
224→ * Returns an instant that is the result of subtracting components of [DateTimePeriod] from this instant. The components
225→ * are subtracted in the order from the largest units to the smallest, i.e. from years to nanoseconds.
226→ *
227→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if this value or the results of intermediate computations are too large to fit in
228→ * [LocalDateTime].
229→ */
230→public fun Instant.minus(period: DateTimePeriod, timeZone: TimeZone): Instant =
231→    /* An overflow can happen for any component, but we are only worried about nanoseconds, as having an overflow in
232→    any other component means that `plus` will throw due to the minimum value of the numeric type overflowing the
233→    platform-specific limits. */
234→    if (period.totalNanoseconds != Long.MIN_VALUE) {
235→        val negatedPeriod = with(period) { buildDateTimePeriod(-totalMonths, -days, -totalNanoseconds) }
236→        plus(negatedPeriod, timeZone)
237→    } else {
238→        val negatedPeriod = with(period) { buildDateTimePeriod(-totalMonths, -days, -(totalNanoseconds+1)) }
239→        plus(negatedPeriod, timeZone).plus(1, DateTimeUnit.NANOSECOND)
240→    }
241→
242→/**
243→ * Returns a [DateTimePeriod] representing the difference between `this` and [other] instants.
244→ *
245→ * The components of [DateTimePeriod] are calculated so that adding it to `this` instant results in the [other] instant.
246→ *
247→ * All components of the [DateTimePeriod] returned are:
248→ * - positive or zero if this instant is earlier than the other,
249→ * - negative or zero if this instant is later than the other,
250→ * - exactly zero if this instant is equal to the other.
251→ *
252→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if `this` or [other] instant is too large to fit in [LocalDateTime].
253→ *     Or (only on the JVM) if the number of months between the two dates exceeds an Int.
254→ */
255→public expect fun Instant.periodUntil(other: Instant, timeZone: TimeZone): DateTimePeriod
256→
257→/**
258→ * Returns the whole number of the specified date or time [units][unit] between `this` and [other] instants
259→ * in the specified [timeZone].
260→ *
261→ * The value returned is:
262→ * - positive or zero if this instant is earlier than the other,
263→ * - negative or zero if this instant is later than the other,
264→ * - zero if this instant is equal to the other.
265→ *
266→ * If the result does not fit in [Long], returns [Long.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Long.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
267→ *
268→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if `this` or [other] instant is too large to fit in [LocalDateTime].
269→ */
270→public expect fun Instant.until(other: Instant, unit: DateTimeUnit, timeZone: TimeZone): Long
271→
272→/**
273→ * Returns the whole number of the specified time [units][unit] between `this` and [other] instants.
274→ *
275→ * The value returned is:
276→ * - positive or zero if this instant is earlier than the other,
277→ * - negative or zero if this instant is later than the other,
278→ * - zero if this instant is equal to the other.
279→ *
280→ * If the result does not fit in [Long], returns [Long.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Long.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
281→ */
282→public fun Instant.until(other: Instant, unit: DateTimeUnit.TimeBased): Long =
283→    try {
284→        multiplyAddAndDivide(other.epochSeconds - epochSeconds,
285→            NANOS_PER_ONE.toLong(),
286→            (other.nanosecondsOfSecond - nanosecondsOfSecond).toLong(),
287→            unit.nanoseconds)
288→    } catch (e: ArithmeticException) {
289→        if (this < other) Long.MAX_VALUE else Long.MIN_VALUE
290→    }
291→
292→/**
293→ * Returns the number of whole days between two instants in the specified [timeZone].
294→ *
295→ * If the result does not fit in [Int], returns [Int.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Int.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
296→ *
297→ * @see Instant.until
298→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if `this` or [other] instant is too large to fit in [LocalDateTime].
299→ */
300→public fun Instant.daysUntil(other: Instant, timeZone: TimeZone): Int =
301→        until(other, DateTimeUnit.DAY, timeZone).clampToInt()
302→
303→/**
304→ * Returns the number of whole months between two instants in the specified [timeZone].
305→ *
306→ * If the result does not fit in [Int], returns [Int.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Int.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
307→ *
308→ * @see Instant.until
309→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if `this` or [other] instant is too large to fit in [LocalDateTime].
310→ */
311→public fun Instant.monthsUntil(other: Instant, timeZone: TimeZone): Int =
312→        until(other, DateTimeUnit.MONTH, timeZone).clampToInt()
313→
314→/**
315→ * Returns the number of whole years between two instants in the specified [timeZone].
316→ *
317→ * If the result does not fit in [Int], returns [Int.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Int.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
318→ *
319→ * @see Instant.until
320→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if `this` or [other] instant is too large to fit in [LocalDateTime].
321→ */
322→public fun Instant.yearsUntil(other: Instant, timeZone: TimeZone): Int =
323→        until(other, DateTimeUnit.YEAR, timeZone).clampToInt()
324→
325→/**
326→ * Returns a [DateTimePeriod] representing the difference between [other] and `this` instants.
327→ *
328→ * The components of [DateTimePeriod] are calculated so that adding it back to the `other` instant results in this instant.
329→ *
330→ * All components of the [DateTimePeriod] returned are:
331→ * - negative or zero if this instant is earlier than the other,
332→ * - positive or zero if this instant is later than the other,
333→ * - exactly zero if this instant is equal to the other.
334→ *
335→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if `this` or [other] instant is too large to fit in [LocalDateTime].
336→ *   Or (only on the JVM) if the number of months between the two dates exceeds an Int.
337→ * @see Instant.periodUntil
338→ */
339→public fun Instant.minus(other: Instant, timeZone: TimeZone): DateTimePeriod =
340→        other.periodUntil(this, timeZone)
341→
342→
343→/**
344→ * Returns an instant that is the result of adding one [unit] to this instant
345→ * in the specified [timeZone].
346→ *
347→ * The returned instant is later than this instant.
348→ *
349→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if this value or the result is too large to fit in [LocalDateTime].
350→ */
351→@Deprecated("Use the plus overload with an explicit number of units", ReplaceWith("this.plus(1, unit, timeZone)"))
352→public expect fun Instant.plus(unit: DateTimeUnit, timeZone: TimeZone): Instant
353→
354→/**
355→ * Returns an instant that is the result of subtracting one [unit] from this instant
356→ * in the specified [timeZone].
357→ *
358→ * The returned instant is earlier than this instant.
359→ *
360→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if this value or the result is too large to fit in [LocalDateTime].
361→ */
362→@Deprecated("Use the minus overload with an explicit number of units", ReplaceWith("this.minus(1, unit, timeZone)"))
363→public fun Instant.minus(unit: DateTimeUnit, timeZone: TimeZone): Instant =
364→    plus(-1, unit, timeZone)
365→
366→/**
367→ * Returns an instant that is the result of adding one [unit] to this instant.
368→ *
369→ * The returned instant is later than this instant.
370→ *
371→ * The return value is clamped to the platform-specific boundaries for [Instant] if the result exceeds them.
372→ */
373→@Deprecated("Use the plus overload with an explicit number of units", ReplaceWith("this.plus(1, unit)"))
374→public fun Instant.plus(unit: DateTimeUnit.TimeBased): Instant =
375→    plus(1L, unit)
376→
377→/**
378→ * Returns an instant that is the result of subtracting one [unit] from this instant.
379→ *
380→ * The returned instant is earlier than this instant.
381→ *
382→ * The return value is clamped to the platform-specific boundaries for [Instant] if the result exceeds them.
383→ */
384→@Deprecated("Use the minus overload with an explicit number of units", ReplaceWith("this.minus(1, unit)"))
385→public fun Instant.minus(unit: DateTimeUnit.TimeBased): Instant =
386→    plus(-1L, unit)
387→
388→/**
389→ * Returns an instant that is the result of adding the [value] number of the specified [unit] to this instant
390→ * in the specified [timeZone].
391→ *
392→ * If the [value] is positive, the returned instant is later than this instant.
393→ * If the [value] is negative, the returned instant is earlier than this instant.
394→ *
395→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if this value or the result is too large to fit in [LocalDateTime].
396→ */
397→public expect fun Instant.plus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit, timeZone: TimeZone): Instant
398→
399→/**
400→ * Returns an instant that is the result of subtracting the [value] number of the specified [unit] from this instant
401→ * in the specified [timeZone].
402→ *
403→ * If the [value] is positive, the returned instant is earlier than this instant.
404→ * If the [value] is negative, the returned instant is later than this instant.
405→ *
406→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if this value or the result is too large to fit in [LocalDateTime].
407→ */
408→public expect fun Instant.minus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit, timeZone: TimeZone): Instant
409→
410→/**
411→ * Returns an instant that is the result of adding the [value] number of the specified [unit] to this instant.
412→ *
413→ * If the [value] is positive, the returned instant is later than this instant.
414→ * If the [value] is negative, the returned instant is earlier than this instant.
415→ *
416→ * The return value is clamped to the platform-specific boundaries for [Instant] if the result exceeds them.
417→ */
418→public fun Instant.plus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit.TimeBased): Instant =
419→    plus(value.toLong(), unit)
420→
421→/**
422→ * Returns an instant that is the result of subtracting the [value] number of the specified [unit] from this instant.
423→ *
424→ * If the [value] is positive, the returned instant is earlier than this instant.
425→ * If the [value] is negative, the returned instant is later than this instant.
426→ *
427→ * The return value is clamped to the platform-specific boundaries for [Instant] if the result exceeds them.
428→ */
429→public fun Instant.minus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit.TimeBased): Instant =
430→    minus(value.toLong(), unit)
431→
432→/**
433→ * Returns an instant that is the result of adding the [value] number of the specified [unit] to this instant
434→ * in the specified [timeZone].
435→ *
436→ * If the [value] is positive, the returned instant is later than this instant.
437→ * If the [value] is negative, the returned instant is earlier than this instant.
438→ *
439→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if this value or the result is too large to fit in [LocalDateTime].
440→ */
441→public expect fun Instant.plus(value: Long, unit: DateTimeUnit, timeZone: TimeZone): Instant
442→
443→/**
444→ * Returns an instant that is the result of subtracting the [value] number of the specified [unit] from this instant
445→ * in the specified [timeZone].
446→ *
447→ * If the [value] is positive, the returned instant is earlier than this instant.
448→ * If the [value] is negative, the returned instant is later than this instant.
449→ *
450→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if this value or the result is too large to fit in [LocalDateTime].
451→ */
452→public fun Instant.minus(value: Long, unit: DateTimeUnit, timeZone: TimeZone): Instant =
453→    if (value != Long.MIN_VALUE) {
454→        plus(-value, unit, timeZone)
455→    } else {
456→        plus(-(value + 1), unit, timeZone).plus(1, unit, timeZone)
457→    }
458→
459→/**
460→ * Returns an instant that is the result of adding the [value] number of the specified [unit] to this instant.
461→ *
462→ * If the [value] is positive, the returned instant is later than this instant.
463→ * If the [value] is negative, the returned instant is earlier than this instant.
464→ *
465→ * The return value is clamped to the platform-specific boundaries for [Instant] if the result exceeds them.
466→ */
467→public expect fun Instant.plus(value: Long, unit: DateTimeUnit.TimeBased): Instant
468→
469→/**
470→ * Returns an instant that is the result of subtracting the [value] number of the specified [unit] from this instant.
471→ *
472→ * If the [value] is positive, the returned instant is earlier than this instant.
473→ * If the [value] is negative, the returned instant is later than this instant.
474→ *
475→ * The return value is clamped to the platform-specific boundaries for [Instant] if the result exceeds them.
476→ */
477→public fun Instant.minus(value: Long, unit: DateTimeUnit.TimeBased): Instant =
478→    if (value != Long.MIN_VALUE) {
479→        plus(-value, unit)
480→    } else {
481→        plus(-(value + 1), unit).plus(1, unit)
482→    }
483→
484→/**
485→ * Returns the whole number of the specified date or time [units][unit] between [other] and `this` instants
486→ * in the specified [timeZone].
487→ *
488→ * The value returned is negative or zero if this instant is earlier than the other,
489→ * and positive or zero if this instant is later than the other.
490→ *
491→ * If the result does not fit in [Long], returns [Long.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Long.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
492→ *
493→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if `this` or [other] instant is too large to fit in [LocalDateTime].
494→ * @see Instant.until
495→ */
496→public fun Instant.minus(other: Instant, unit: DateTimeUnit, timeZone: TimeZone): Long =
497→        other.until(this, unit, timeZone)
498→
499→/**
500→ * Returns the whole number of the specified time [units][unit] between [other] and `this` instants.
501→ *
502→ * The value returned is negative or zero if this instant is earlier than the other,
503→ * and positive or zero if this instant is later than the other.
504→ *
505→ * If the result does not fit in [Long], returns [Long.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Long.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
506→ *
507→ * @see Instant.until
508→ */
509→public fun Instant.minus(other: Instant, unit: DateTimeUnit.TimeBased): Long =
510→    other.until(this, unit)
511→
512→/**
513→ * Formats this value using the given [format] using the given [offset].
514→ *
515→ * Equivalent to calling [DateTimeFormat.format] on [format] and using [DateTimeComponents.setDateTimeOffset] in
516→ * the lambda.
517→ *
518→ * [DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET] is a format very similar to the one used by [toString].
519→ * The only difference is that [Instant.toString] adds trailing zeros to the fraction-of-second component so that the
520→ * number of digits after a dot is a multiple of three.
521→ */
522→public fun Instant.format(format: DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents>, offset: UtcOffset = UtcOffset.ZERO): String {
523→    val instant = this
524→    return format.format { setDateTimeOffset(instant, offset) }
525→}

1→/*
  2→ * Copyright 2019-2022 JetBrains s.r.o. and contributors.
  3→ * Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 License that can be found in the LICENSE.txt file.
  4→ */
  5→
  6→package kotlinx.datetime
  7→
  8→import kotlinx.datetime.LocalDate.Companion.parse
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
 10→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.LocalDateTimeIso8601Serializer
 11→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 12→
 13→/**
 14→ * The representation of a specific civil date and time without a reference to a particular time zone.
 15→ *
 16→ * This class does not describe specific *moments in time*, which are represented as [Instant] values.
 17→ * Instead, its instances can be thought of as clock readings, something that an observer in a particular time zone
 18→ * could witness.
 19→ * For example, `2020-08-30T18:43` is not a *moment in time*, since someone in Berlin and someone in Tokyo would witness
 20→ * this on their clocks at different times.
 21→ *
 22→ * The main purpose of this class is to provide human-readable representations of [Instant] values, or to transfer them
 23→ * as data.
 24→ *
 25→ * The arithmetic on [LocalDateTime] values is not provided, since without accounting for the time zone transitions it may give misleading results.
 26→ */
 27→@Serializable(with = LocalDateTimeIso8601Serializer::class)
 28→public expect class LocalDateTime : Comparable<LocalDateTime> {
 29→    public companion object {
 30→
 31→        /**
 32→         * A shortcut for calling [DateTimeFormat.parse].
 33→         *
 34→         * Parses a string that represents a date/time value including date and time components
 35→         * but without any time zone component and returns the parsed [LocalDateTime] value.
 36→         *
 37→         * If [format] is not specified, [Formats.ISO] is used.
 38→         *
 39→         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text cannot be parsed or the boundaries of [LocalDateTime] are
 40→         * exceeded.
 41→         */
 42→        public fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDateTime> = getIsoDateTimeFormat()): LocalDateTime
 43→
 44→        /**
 45→         * Creates a new format for parsing and formatting [LocalDateTime] values.
 46→         *
 47→         * Examples:
 48→         * ```
 49→         * // `2020-08-30 18:43:13`, using predefined date and time formats
 50→         * LocalDateTime.Format { date(LocalDate.Formats.ISO); char(' '); time(LocalTime.Formats.ISO) }
 51→         *
 52→         * // `08/30 18:43:13`, using a custom format:
 53→         * LocalDateTime.Format {
 54→         *   monthNumber(); char('/'); dayOfMonth()
 55→         *   char(' ')
 56→         *   hour(); char(':'); minute()
 57→         *   optional { char(':'); second() }
 58→         * }
 59→         * ```
 60→         *
 61→         * Only parsing and formatting of well-formed values is supported. If the input does not fit the boundaries
 62→         * (for example, [dayOfMonth] is 31 for February), consider using [DateTimeComponents.Format] instead.
 63→         *
 64→         * There is a collection of predefined formats in [LocalDateTime.Formats].
 65→         *
 66→         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if parsing using this format is ambiguous.
 67→         */
 68→        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 69→        public fun Format(builder: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTime.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalDateTime>
 70→
 71→        internal val MIN: LocalDateTime
 72→        internal val MAX: LocalDateTime
 73→    }
 74→
 75→    /**
 76→     * A collection of predefined formats for parsing and formatting [LocalDateTime] values.
 77→     *
 78→     * [LocalDateTime.Formats.ISO] is a popular predefined format.
 79→     *
 80→     * If predefined formats are not sufficient, use [LocalDateTime.Format] to create a custom
 81→     * [kotlinx.datetime.format.DateTimeFormat] for [LocalDateTime] values.
 82→     */
 83→    public object Formats {
 84→        /**
 85→         * ISO 8601 extended format.
 86→         *
 87→         * Examples of date/time in ISO 8601 format:
 88→         * - `2020-08-30T18:43`
 89→         * - `+12020-08-30T18:43:00`
 90→         * - `0000-08-30T18:43:00.5`
 91→         * - `-0001-08-30T18:43:00.123456789`
 92→         *
 93→         * When formatting, seconds are always included, even if they are zero.
 94→         * Fractional parts of the second are included if non-zero.
 95→         *
 96→         * Guaranteed to parse all strings that [LocalDateTime.toString] produces.
 97→         */
 98→        public val ISO: DateTimeFormat<LocalDateTime>
 99→    }
100→
101→    /**
102→     * Constructs a [LocalDateTime] instance from the given date and time components.
103→     *
104→     * The components [monthNumber] and [dayOfMonth] are 1-based.
105→     *
106→     * The supported ranges of components:
107→     * - [year] the range is platform dependent, but at least is enough to represent dates of all instants between
108→     *          [Instant.DISTANT_PAST] and [Instant.DISTANT_FUTURE]
109→     * - [monthNumber] `1..12`
110→     * - [dayOfMonth] `1..31`, the upper bound can be less, depending on the month
111→     * - [hour] `0..23`
112→     * - [minute] `0..59`
113→     * - [second] `0..59`
114→     * - [nanosecond] `0..999_999_999`
115→     *
116→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any parameter is out of range, or if [dayOfMonth] is invalid for the given [monthNumber] and
117→     * [year].
118→     */
119→    public constructor(
120→        year: Int,
121→        monthNumber: Int,
122→        dayOfMonth: Int,
123→        hour: Int,
124→        minute: Int,
125→        second: Int = 0,
126→        nanosecond: Int = 0
127→    )
128→
129→    /**
130→     * Constructs a [LocalDateTime] instance from the given date and time components.
131→     *
132→     * The supported ranges of components:
133→     * - [year] the range is platform dependent, but at least is enough to represent dates of all instants between
134→     *          [Instant.DISTANT_PAST] and [Instant.DISTANT_FUTURE]
135→     * - [month] all values of the [Month] enum
136→     * - [dayOfMonth] `1..31`, the upper bound can be less, depending on the month
137→     * - [hour] `0..23`
138→     * - [minute] `0..59`
139→     * - [second] `0..59`
140→     * - [nanosecond] `0..999_999_999`
141→     *
142→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any parameter is out of range, or if [dayOfMonth] is invalid for the given [month] and
143→     * [year].
144→     */
145→    public constructor(
146→        year: Int,
147→        month: Month,
148→        dayOfMonth: Int,
149→        hour: Int,
150→        minute: Int,
151→        second: Int = 0,
152→        nanosecond: Int = 0
153→    )
154→
155→    /**
156→     * Constructs a [LocalDateTime] instance by combining the given [date] and [time] parts.
157→     */
158→    public constructor(date: LocalDate, time: LocalTime)
159→
160→    /** Returns the year component of the date. */
161→    public val year: Int
162→
163→    /** Returns the number-of-month (1..12) component of the date. */
164→    public val monthNumber: Int
165→
166→    /** Returns the month ([Month]) component of the date. */
167→    public val month: Month
168→
169→    /** Returns the day-of-month component of the date. */
170→    public val dayOfMonth: Int
171→
172→    /** Returns the day-of-week component of the date. */
173→    public val dayOfWeek: DayOfWeek
174→
175→    /** Returns the day-of-year component of the date. */
176→    public val dayOfYear: Int
177→
178→    /** Returns the hour-of-day time component of this date/time value. */
179→    public val hour: Int
180→
181→    /** Returns the minute-of-hour time component of this date/time value. */
182→    public val minute: Int
183→
184→    /** Returns the second-of-minute time component of this date/time value. */
185→    public val second: Int
186→
187→    /** Returns the nanosecond-of-second time component of this date/time value. */
188→    public val nanosecond: Int
189→
190→    /** Returns the date part of this date/time value. */
191→    public val date: LocalDate
192→
193→    /** Returns the time part of this date/time value. */
194→    public val time: LocalTime
195→
196→    /**
197→     * Compares `this` date/time value with the [other] date/time value.
198→     * Returns zero if this value is equal to the other,
199→     * a negative number if this value represents earlier civil time than the other,
200→     * and a positive number if this value represents later civil time than the other.
201→     */
202→    // TODO: add a note about pitfalls of comparing localdatetimes falling in the Autumn transition
203→    public override operator fun compareTo(other: LocalDateTime): Int
204→
205→    /**
206→     * Converts this date/time value to the ISO 8601 string representation.
207→     *
208→     * For readability, if the time represents a round minute (without seconds or fractional seconds),
209→     * the string representation will not include seconds. Also, fractions of seconds will add trailing zeros to
210→     * the fractional part until its length is a multiple of three.
211→     *
212→     * Examples of output:
213→     * - `2020-08-30T18:43`
214→     * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00`
215→     * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00.500`
216→     * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00.123456789`
217→     *
218→     * @see LocalTime.toString for details of how the time part is formatted.
219→     * @see Formats.ISO for a very similar format. The difference is that [Formats.ISO] will always include seconds,
220→     * even if they are zero, and will not add trailing zeros to the fractional part of the second for readability.
221→     * @see parse for the dual operation: obtaining [LocalDateTime] from a string.
222→     * @see LocalDateTime.format for formatting using a custom format.
223→     */
224→    public override fun toString(): String
225→}
226→
227→/**
228→ * Formats this value using the given [format].
229→ * Equivalent to calling [DateTimeFormat.format] on [format] with `this`.
230→ */
231→public fun LocalDateTime.format(format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDateTime>): String = format.format(this)
232→
233→/**
234→ * @suppress
235→ */
236→@Deprecated("Removed to support more idiomatic code. See https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime/issues/339", ReplaceWith("LocalDateTime.parse(this)"), DeprecationLevel.WARNING)
237→public fun String.toLocalDateTime(): LocalDateTime = LocalDateTime.parse(this)
238→
239→// workaround for https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-65484
240→internal fun getIsoDateTimeFormat() = LocalDateTime.Formats.ISO
241→

  9→package kotlinx.datetime
 10→
 11→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.*
 12→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 13→
 14→/**
 15→ * A time zone, provides the conversion between [Instant] and [LocalDateTime] values
 16→ * using a collection of rules specifying which [LocalDateTime] value corresponds to each [Instant].
 17→ */
 18→@Serializable(with = TimeZoneSerializer::class)
 19→public expect open class TimeZone {
 20→    /**
 21→     * Returns the identifier string of the time zone.
 22→     *
 23→     * This identifier can be used later for finding this time zone with [TimeZone.of] function.
 24→     */
 25→    public val id: String
 26→
 27→    // TODO: Declare and document toString/equals/hashCode
 28→
 29→    public companion object {
 30→        /**
 31→         * Queries the current system time zone.
 32→         *
 33→         * If the current system time zone changes, this function can reflect this change on the next invocation.
 34→         */
 35→        public fun currentSystemDefault(): TimeZone
 36→
 37→        /**
 38→         * Returns the time zone with the fixed UTC+0 offset.
 39→         */
 40→        public val UTC: FixedOffsetTimeZone
 41→
 42→        /**
 43→         * Returns the time zone identified by the provided [zoneId].
 44→         *
 45→         * The supported variants of time zone identifiers:
 46→         * - `Z`, 'UTC', 'UT' or 'GMT' — identifies the fixed-offset time zone [TimeZone.UTC],
 47→         * - a string starting with '+', '-', `UTC+`, `UTC-`, `UT+`, `UT-`, `GMT+`, `GMT-` — identifiers the time zone
 48→         *   with the fixed offset specified after `+` or `-`,
 49→         * - all other strings are treated as region-based zone identifiers.
 50→         * In the IANA Time Zone Database (TZDB) which is used as the default source of time zones,
 51→         * these ids are usually in the form `area/city`, for example, `Europe/Berlin` or `America/Los_Angeles`.
 52→         *
 53→         * @throws IllegalTimeZoneException if [zoneId] has an invalid format or a time-zone with the name [zoneId]
 54→         * is not found.
 55→         */
 56→        public fun of(zoneId: String): TimeZone
 57→
 58→        /**
 59→         * Queries the set of identifiers of time zones available in the system.
 60→         */
 61→        public val availableZoneIds: Set<String>
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    /**
 65→     * Return the civil date/time value that this instant has in the time zone provided as an implicit receiver.
 66→     *
 67→     * Note that while this conversion is unambiguous, the inverse ([LocalDateTime.toInstant])
 68→     * is not necessary so.
 69→     *
 70→     * @see LocalDateTime.toInstant
 71→     * @see Instant.offsetIn
 72→     * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if this value is too large to fit in [LocalDateTime].
 73→     */
 74→    public fun Instant.toLocalDateTime(): LocalDateTime
 75→
 76→    /**
 77→     * Returns an instant that corresponds to this civil date/time value in the time zone provided as an implicit receiver.
 78→     *
 79→     * Note that the conversion is not always unambiguous. There can be the following possible situations:
 80→     * - There's only one instant that has this date/time value in the time zone. In this case
 81→     * the conversion is unambiguous.
 82→     * - There's no instant that has this date/time value in the time zone. Such situation appears when
 83→     * the time zone experiences a transition from a lesser to a greater offset. In this case the conversion is performed with
 84→     * the lesser offset.
 85→     * - There are two possible instants that can have this date/time components in the time zone. In this case the earlier
 86→     * instant is returned.
 87→     *
 88→     * @see Instant.toLocalDateTime
 89→     */
 90→    public fun LocalDateTime.toInstant(): Instant
 91→}
 92→
 93→/**
 94→ * A time zone that is known to always have the same offset from UTC.
 95→ */
 96→@Serializable(with = FixedOffsetTimeZoneSerializer::class)
 97→public expect class FixedOffsetTimeZone : TimeZone {
 98→    public constructor(offset: UtcOffset)
 99→
100→    /**
101→     * The constant offset from UTC that this time zone has.
102→     */
103→    public val offset: UtcOffset
104→
105→    @Deprecated("Use offset.totalSeconds", ReplaceWith("offset.totalSeconds"))
106→    public val totalSeconds: Int
107→}
108→
109→@Deprecated("Use FixedOffsetTimeZone of UtcOffset instead", ReplaceWith("FixedOffsetTimeZone"))
110→public typealias ZoneOffset = FixedOffsetTimeZone
111→
112→/**
113→ * Finds the offset from UTC this time zone has at the specified [instant] of physical time.
114→ *
115→ * @see Instant.toLocalDateTime
116→ * @see TimeZone.offsetAt
117→ */
118→public expect fun TimeZone.offsetAt(instant: Instant): UtcOffset
119→
120→/**
121→ * Returns a civil date/time value that this instant has in the specified [timeZone].
122→ *
123→ * Note that while this conversion is unambiguous, the inverse ([LocalDateTime.toInstant])
124→ * is not necessary so.
125→ *
126→ * @see LocalDateTime.toInstant
127→ * @see Instant.offsetIn
128→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if this value is too large to fit in [LocalDateTime].
129→ */
130→public expect fun Instant.toLocalDateTime(timeZone: TimeZone): LocalDateTime
131→
132→/**
133→ * Returns a civil date/time value that this instant has in the specified [UTC offset][offset].
134→ *
135→ * @see LocalDateTime.toInstant
136→ * @see Instant.offsetIn
137→ */
138→internal expect fun Instant.toLocalDateTime(offset: UtcOffset): LocalDateTime
139→
140→/**
141→ * Finds the offset from UTC the specified [timeZone] has at this instant of physical time.
142→ *
143→ * @see Instant.toLocalDateTime
144→ * @see TimeZone.offsetAt
145→ */
146→public fun Instant.offsetIn(timeZone: TimeZone): UtcOffset =
147→        timeZone.offsetAt(this)
148→
149→/**
150→ * Returns an instant that corresponds to this civil date/time value in the specified [timeZone].
151→ *
152→ * Note that the conversion is not always unambiguous. There can be the following possible situations:
153→ * - There's only one instant that has this date/time value in the [timeZone]. In this case
154→ * the conversion is unambiguous.
155→ * - There's no instant that has this date/time value in the [timeZone]. Such situation appears when
156→ * the time zone experiences a transition from a lesser to a greater offset. In this case the conversion is performed with
157→ * the lesser offset.
158→ * - There are two possible instants that can have this date/time components in the [timeZone]. In this case the earlier
159→ * instant is returned.
160→ *
161→ * @see Instant.toLocalDateTime
162→ */
163→public expect fun LocalDateTime.toInstant(timeZone: TimeZone): Instant
164→
165→/**
166→ * Returns an instant that corresponds to this civil date/time value that happens at the specified [UTC offset][offset].
167→ *
168→ * @see Instant.toLocalDateTime
169→ */
170→public expect fun LocalDateTime.toInstant(offset: UtcOffset): Instant
171→
172→/**
173→ * Returns an instant that corresponds to the start of this date in the specified [timeZone].
174→ *
175→ * Note that it's not equivalent to `atTime(0, 0).toInstant(timeZone)`
176→ * because a day does not always start at the fixed time 0:00:00.
177→ * For example, if due do daylight saving time, clocks were shifted from 23:30
178→ * of one day directly to 00:30 of the next day, skipping the midnight, then
179→ * `atStartOfDayIn` would return the `Instant` corresponding to 00:30, whereas
180→ * `atTime(0, 0).toInstant(timeZone)` would return the `Instant` corresponding
181→ * to 01:00.
182→ */
183→public expect fun LocalDate.atStartOfDayIn(timeZone: TimeZone): Instant
184→

 5→
 6→package kotlinx.datetime
 7→
 8→import kotlin.time.*
 9→
10→/**
11→ * A source of [Instant] values.
12→ *
13→ * See [Clock.System][Clock.System] for the clock instance that queries the operating system.
14→ */
15→public interface Clock {
16→    /**
17→     * Returns the [Instant] corresponding to the current time, according to this clock.
18→     */
19→    public fun now(): Instant
20→
21→    /**
22→     * The [Clock] instance that queries the operating system as its source of knowledge of time.
23→     */
24→    public object System : Clock {
25→        override fun now(): Instant = @Suppress("DEPRECATION_ERROR") Instant.now()
26→    }
27→
28→    public companion object {
29→
30→    }
31→}
32→
33→/**
34→ * Returns the current date at the given [time zone][timeZone], according to [this Clock][this].
35→ */
36→public fun Clock.todayIn(timeZone: TimeZone): LocalDate =
37→    now().toLocalDateTime(timeZone).date
38→
39→/**
40→ * Returns a [TimeSource] that uses this [Clock] to mark a time instant and to find the amount of time elapsed since that mark.
41→ */
42→@ExperimentalTime
43→public fun Clock.asTimeSource(): TimeSource.WithComparableMarks = object : TimeSource.WithComparableMarks {
44→    override fun markNow(): ComparableTimeMark = InstantTimeMark(now(), this@asTimeSource)
45→}
46→
47→@ExperimentalTime
48→private class InstantTimeMark(private val instant: Instant, private val clock: Clock) : ComparableTimeMark {
49→    override fun elapsedNow(): Duration = saturatingDiff(clock.now(), instant)
50→
51→    override fun plus(duration: Duration): ComparableTimeMark = InstantTimeMark(instant.saturatingAdd(duration), clock)
52→    override fun minus(duration: Duration): ComparableTimeMark = InstantTimeMark(instant.saturatingAdd(-duration), clock)
53→
54→    override fun minus(other: ComparableTimeMark): Duration {
55→        if (other !is InstantTimeMark || other.clock != this.clock) {
56→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Subtracting or comparing time marks from different time sources is not possible: $this and $other")
57→        }
58→        return saturatingDiff(this.instant, other.instant)
59→    }
60→
61→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
62→        return other is InstantTimeMark && this.clock == other.clock && this.instant == other.instant
63→    }
64→
65→    override fun hashCode(): Int = instant.hashCode()
66→
67→    override fun toString(): String = "InstantTimeMark($instant, $clock)"
68→
69→    private fun Instant.isSaturated() = this == Instant.MAX || this == Instant.MIN
70→    private fun Instant.saturatingAdd(duration: Duration): Instant {
71→        if (isSaturated()) {
72→            if (duration.isInfinite() && duration.isPositive() != this.isDistantFuture) {
73→                throw IllegalArgumentException("Summing infinities of different signs")
74→            }
75→            return this
76→        }
77→        return this + duration
78→    }
79→    private fun saturatingDiff(instant1: Instant, instant2: Instant): Duration = when {
80→        instant1 == instant2 ->
81→            Duration.ZERO
82→        instant1.isSaturated() || instant2.isSaturated() ->
83→            (instant1 - instant2) * Double.POSITIVE_INFINITY
84→        else ->
85→            instant1 - instant2
86→    }
87→}
88→
89→@Deprecated("Use Clock.todayIn instead", ReplaceWith("this.todayIn(timeZone)"), DeprecationLevel.WARNING)
90→public fun Clock.todayAt(timeZone: TimeZone): LocalDate = todayIn(timeZone)
91→

  6→package kotlinx.datetime
  7→
  8→import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.LocalDateIso8601Serializer
 10→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 11→
 12→/**
 13→ * The date part of [LocalDateTime].
 14→ *
 15→ * This class represents dates without a reference to a particular time zone.
 16→ * As such, these objects may denote different spans of time in different time zones: for someone in Berlin,
 17→ * `2020-08-30` started and ended at different moments from those for someone in Tokyo.
 18→ *
 19→ * The arithmetic on [LocalDate] values is defined independently of the time zone (so `2020-08-30` plus one day
 20→ * is `2020-08-31` everywhere): see various [LocalDate.plus] and [LocalDate.minus] functions, as well
 21→ * as [LocalDate.periodUntil] and various other [*until][LocalDate.daysUntil] functions.
 22→ */
 23→@Serializable(with = LocalDateIso8601Serializer::class)
 24→public expect class LocalDate : Comparable<LocalDate> {
 25→    public companion object {
 26→        /**
 27→         * A shortcut for calling [DateTimeFormat.parse].
 28→         *
 29→         * Parses a string that represents a date and returns the parsed [LocalDate] value.
 30→         *
 31→         * If [format] is not specified, [Formats.ISO] is used.
 32→         *
 33→         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text cannot be parsed or the boundaries of [LocalDate] are exceeded.
 34→         *
 35→         * @see LocalDate.toString for formatting using the default format.
 36→         * @see LocalDate.format for formatting using a custom format.
 37→         */
 38→        public fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> = getIsoDateFormat()): LocalDate
 39→
 40→        /**
 41→         * Returns a [LocalDate] that is [epochDays] number of days from the epoch day `1970-01-01`.
 42→         *
 43→         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the result exceeds the platform-specific boundaries of [LocalDate].
 44→         *
 45→         * @see LocalDate.toEpochDays
 46→         */
 47→        public fun fromEpochDays(epochDays: Int): LocalDate
 48→
 49→        /**
 50→         * Creates a new format for parsing and formatting [LocalDate] values.
 51→         *
 52→         * Example:
 53→         * ```
 54→         * // 2020 Jan 05
 55→         * LocalDate.Format {
 56→         *   year()
 57→         *   char(' ')
 58→         *   monthName(MonthNames.ENGLISH_ABBREVIATED)
 59→         *   char(' ')
 60→         *   dayOfMonth()
 61→         * }
 62→         * ```
 63→         *
 64→         * Only parsing and formatting of well-formed values is supported. If the input does not fit the boundaries
 65→         * (for example, [dayOfMonth] is 31 for February), consider using [DateTimeComponents.Format] instead.
 66→         *
 67→         * There is a collection of predefined formats in [LocalDate.Formats].
 68→         *
 69→         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if parsing using this format is ambiguous.
 70→         */
 71→        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 72→        public fun Format(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>
 73→
 74→        internal val MIN: LocalDate
 75→        internal val MAX: LocalDate
 76→    }
 77→
 78→    /**
 79→     * A collection of predefined formats for parsing and formatting [LocalDate] values.
 80→     *
 81→     * See [LocalDate.Formats.ISO] and [LocalDate.Formats.ISO_BASIC] for popular predefined formats.
 82→     * [LocalDate.parse] and [LocalDate.toString] can be used as convenient shortcuts for the
 83→     * [LocalDate.Formats.ISO] format.
 84→     *
 85→     * If predefined formats are not sufficient, use [LocalDate.Format] to create a custom
 86→     * [kotlinx.datetime.format.DateTimeFormat] for [LocalDate] values.
 87→     */
 88→    public object Formats {
 89→        /**
 90→         * ISO 8601 extended format, which is the format used by [LocalDate.toString] and [LocalDate.parse].
 91→         *
 92→         * Examples of dates in ISO 8601 format:
 93→         * - `2020-08-30`
 94→         * - `+12020-08-30`
 95→         * - `0000-08-30`
 96→         * - `-0001-08-30`
 97→         */
 98→        public val ISO: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>
 99→
100→        /**
101→         * ISO 8601 basic format.
102→         *
103→         * Examples of dates in ISO 8601 basic format:
104→         * - `20200830`
105→         * - `+120200830`
106→         * - `00000830`
107→         * - `-00010830`
108→         */
109→        public val ISO_BASIC: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>
110→    }
111→
112→    /**
113→     * Constructs a [LocalDate] instance from the given date components.
114→     *
115→     * The components [monthNumber] and [dayOfMonth] are 1-based.
116→     *
117→     * The supported ranges of components:
118→     * - [year] the range is platform dependent, but at least is enough to represent dates of all instants between
119→     *          [Instant.DISTANT_PAST] and [Instant.DISTANT_FUTURE]
120→     * - [monthNumber] `1..12`
121→     * - [dayOfMonth] `1..31`, the upper bound can be less, depending on the month
122→     *
123→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any parameter is out of range, or if [dayOfMonth] is invalid for the
124→     * given [monthNumber] and [year].
125→     */
126→    public constructor(year: Int, monthNumber: Int, dayOfMonth: Int)
127→
128→    /**
129→     * Constructs a [LocalDate] instance from the given date components.
130→     *
131→     * The supported ranges of components:
132→     * - [year] the range is platform dependent, but at least is enough to represent dates of all instants between
133→     *          [Instant.DISTANT_PAST] and [Instant.DISTANT_FUTURE]
134→     * - [month] all values of the [Month] enum
135→     * - [dayOfMonth] `1..31`, the upper bound can be less, depending on the month
136→     *
137→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any parameter is out of range, or if [dayOfMonth] is invalid for the
138→     * given [month] and [year].
139→     */
140→    public constructor(year: Int, month: Month, dayOfMonth: Int)
141→
142→    /** Returns the year component of the date. */
143→    public val year: Int
144→
145→    /** Returns the number-of-month (1..12) component of the date. */
146→    public val monthNumber: Int
147→
148→    /** Returns the month ([Month]) component of the date. */
149→    public val month: Month
150→
151→    /** Returns the day-of-month component of the date. */
152→    public val dayOfMonth: Int
153→
154→    /** Returns the day-of-week component of the date. */
155→    public val dayOfWeek: DayOfWeek
156→
157→    /** Returns the day-of-year component of the date. */
158→    public val dayOfYear: Int
159→
160→    /**
161→     * Returns the number of days since the epoch day `1970-01-01`.
162→     *
163→     * If the result does not fit in [Int], returns [Int.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Int.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
164→     *
165→     * @see LocalDate.fromEpochDays
166→     */
167→    public fun toEpochDays(): Int
168→
169→    /**
170→     * Compares `this` date with the [other] date.
171→     * Returns zero if this date represent the same day as the other (i.e. equal to other),
172→     * a negative number if this date is earlier than the other,
173→     * and a positive number if this date is later than the other.
174→     */
175→    public override fun compareTo(other: LocalDate): Int
176→
177→    /**
178→     * Converts this date to the extended ISO-8601 string representation.
179→     *
180→     * @see Formats.ISO for the format details.
181→     * @see parse for the dual operation: obtaining [LocalDate] from a string.
182→     * @see LocalDate.format for formatting using a custom format.
183→     */
184→    public override fun toString(): String
185→}
186→
187→/**
188→ * Formats this value using the given [format].
189→ * Equivalent to calling [DateTimeFormat.format] on [format] with `this`.
190→ */
191→public fun LocalDate.format(format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>): String = format.format(this)
192→
193→/**
194→ * @suppress
195→ */
196→@Deprecated("Removed to support more idiomatic code. See https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime/issues/339", ReplaceWith("LocalDate.parse(this)"), DeprecationLevel.WARNING)
197→public fun String.toLocalDate(): LocalDate = LocalDate.parse(this)
198→
199→/**
200→ * Combines this date's components with the specified time components into a [LocalDateTime] value.
201→ *
202→ * For finding an instant that corresponds to the start of a date in a particular time zone consider using
203→ * [LocalDate.atStartOfDayIn] function because a day does not always start at the fixed time 0:00:00.
204→ */
205→public fun LocalDate.atTime(hour: Int, minute: Int, second: Int = 0, nanosecond: Int = 0): LocalDateTime =
206→    LocalDateTime(year, monthNumber, dayOfMonth, hour, minute, second, nanosecond)
207→
208→/**
209→ * Combines this date's components with the specified [LocalTime] components into a [LocalDateTime] value.
210→ *
211→ * For finding an instant that corresponds to the start of a date in a particular time zone consider using
212→ * [LocalDate.atStartOfDayIn] function because a day does not always start at the fixed time 0:00:00.
213→ */
214→public fun LocalDate.atTime(time: LocalTime): LocalDateTime = LocalDateTime(this, time)
215→
216→
217→/**
218→ * Returns a date that is the result of adding components of [DatePeriod] to this date. The components are
219→ * added in the order from the largest units to the smallest, i.e. from years to days.
220→ *
221→ * @see LocalDate.periodUntil
222→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if this value or the results of intermediate computations are too large to fit in
223→ * [LocalDate].
224→ */
225→public expect operator fun LocalDate.plus(period: DatePeriod): LocalDate
226→
227→/**
228→ * Returns a date that is the result of subtracting components of [DatePeriod] from this date. The components are
229→ * subtracted in the order from the largest units to the smallest, i.e. from years to days.
230→ *
231→ * @see LocalDate.periodUntil
232→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if this value or the results of intermediate computations are too large to fit in
233→ * [LocalDate].
234→ */
235→public operator fun LocalDate.minus(period: DatePeriod): LocalDate =
236→    if (period.days != Int.MIN_VALUE && period.months != Int.MIN_VALUE) {
237→        plus(with(period) { DatePeriod(-years, -months, -days) })
238→    } else {
239→        minus(period.years, DateTimeUnit.YEAR).minus(period.months, DateTimeUnit.MONTH)
240→            .minus(period.days, DateTimeUnit.DAY)
241→    }
242→
243→/**
244→ * Returns a [DatePeriod] representing the difference between `this` and [other] dates.
245→ *
246→ * The components of [DatePeriod] are calculated so that adding it to `this` date results in the [other] date.
247→ *
248→ * All components of the [DatePeriod] returned are:
249→ * - positive or zero if this date is earlier than the other,
250→ * - negative or zero if this date is later than the other,
251→ * - exactly zero if this date is equal to the other.
252→ *
253→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if the number of months between the two dates exceeds an Int (JVM only).
254→ *
255→ * @see LocalDate.minus
256→ */
257→public expect fun LocalDate.periodUntil(other: LocalDate): DatePeriod
258→
259→/**
260→ * Returns a [DatePeriod] representing the difference between [other] and `this` dates.
261→ *
262→ * The components of [DatePeriod] are calculated so that adding it back to the `other` date results in this date.
263→ *
264→ * All components of the [DatePeriod] returned are:
265→ * - negative or zero if this date is earlier than the other,
266→ * - positive or zero if this date is later than the other,
267→ * - exactly zero if this date is equal to the other.
268→ *
269→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if the number of months between the two dates exceeds an Int (JVM only).
270→ *
271→ * @see LocalDate.periodUntil
272→ */
273→public operator fun LocalDate.minus(other: LocalDate): DatePeriod = other.periodUntil(this)
274→
275→/**
276→ * Returns the whole number of the specified date [units][unit] between `this` and [other] dates.
277→ *
278→ * The value returned is:
279→ * - positive or zero if this date is earlier than the other,
280→ * - negative or zero if this date is later than the other,
281→ * - zero if this date is equal to the other.
282→
283→ * If the result does not fit in [Int], returns [Int.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Int.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
284→ *
285→ * @see LocalDate.daysUntil
286→ * @see LocalDate.monthsUntil
287→ * @see LocalDate.yearsUntil
288→ *
289→ */
290→public expect fun LocalDate.until(other: LocalDate, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): Int
291→
292→/**
293→ * Returns the number of whole days between two dates.
294→ *
295→ * If the result does not fit in [Int], returns [Int.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Int.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
296→ *
297→ * @see LocalDate.until
298→ */
299→public expect fun LocalDate.daysUntil(other: LocalDate): Int
300→
301→/**
302→ * Returns the number of whole months between two dates.
303→ *
304→ * If the result does not fit in [Int], returns [Int.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Int.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
305→ *
306→ * @see LocalDate.until
307→ */
308→public expect fun LocalDate.monthsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int
309→
310→/**
311→ * Returns the number of whole years between two dates.
312→ *
313→ * If the result does not fit in [Int], returns [Int.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Int.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
314→ *
315→ * @see LocalDate.until
316→ */
317→public expect fun LocalDate.yearsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int
318→
319→/**

10→import kotlinx.cinterop.*
11→import platform.Foundation.*
12→
13→/**
14→ * Converts the [Instant] to an instance of [NSDate].
15→ *
16→ * The conversion is lossy: Darwin uses millisecond precision to represent dates, and [Instant] allows for nanosecond
17→ * resolution.
18→ */
19→public fun Instant.toNSDate(): NSDate {
20→    val secs = epochSeconds * 1.0 + nanosecondsOfSecond / 1.0e9
21→    if (secs < NSDate.distantPast.timeIntervalSince1970 || secs > NSDate.distantFuture.timeIntervalSince1970) {
22→        throw IllegalArgumentException("Boundaries of NSDate exceeded")
23→    }
24→    return NSDate.dateWithTimeIntervalSince1970(secs)
25→}
26→
27→/**
28→ * Converts the [NSDate] to the corresponding [Instant].
29→ *
30→ * Even though Darwin only uses millisecond precision, it is possible that [date] uses larger resolution, storing
31→ * microseconds or even nanoseconds. In this case, the sub-millisecond parts of [date] are rounded to the nearest
32→ * millisecond, given that they are likely to be conversion artifacts.
33→ */
34→public fun NSDate.toKotlinInstant(): Instant {
35→    val secs = timeIntervalSince1970()
36→    val millis = secs * 1000 + if (secs > 0) 0.5 else -0.5
37→    return Instant.fromEpochMilliseconds(millis.toLong())
38→}
39→
40→/**
41→ * Converts the [TimeZone] to [NSTimeZone].
42→ *
43→ * If the time zone is represented as a fixed number of seconds from UTC+0 (for example, if it is the result of a call
44→ * to [TimeZone.offset]) and the offset is not given in even minutes but also includes seconds, this method throws
45→ * [IllegalArgumentException] to denote that lossy conversion would happen, as Darwin internally rounds the offsets
46→ * to the nearest minute.
47→ *
48→ * If the time zone is unknown to the Foundation framework, [IllegalArgumentException] will be thrown.
49→ */
50→public fun TimeZone.toNSTimeZone(): NSTimeZone = if (this is FixedOffsetTimeZone) {
51→    require(offset.totalSeconds % 60 == 0) {
52→        "NSTimeZone cannot represent fixed-offset time zones with offsets not expressed in whole minutes: $this"
53→    }
54→    NSTimeZone.timeZoneForSecondsFromGMT(offset.totalSeconds.convert())
55→} else {
56→    NSTimeZone.timeZoneWithName(id)
57→        ?: NSTimeZone.timeZoneWithAbbreviation(id)
58→        ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("The Foundation framework does not support the timezone '$id'")
59→}
60→

 * A time zone, provides the conversion between [Instant] and [LocalDateTime] values
 * using a collection of rules specifying which [LocalDateTime] value corresponds to each [Instant].

@Serializable(with = TimeZoneSerializer::class)
public expect open class TimeZone {
","new_string":"/**
 * Describes how to convert between [Instant] values on the global timeline and human-readable [LocalDateTime] values
 * for a particular geographic region or a fixed [UtcOffset].
 *
 * A time zone encapsulates the daylight-saving and historical transition rules (usually sourced from the IANA Time Zone
 * Database) that tell which local time corresponds to every instant. Use it whenever you need to present an instant to
 * a user, convert user input back into an instant, or compare civil times that depend on a location.
 *
 * ## Acquiring a time zone
 * - Follow the host/device settings with [TimeZone.currentSystemDefault].
 * - Resolve a region such as `"Europe/Berlin"` or a custom offset like `"UTC+02:30"` with [TimeZone.of].
 * - Enumerate [availableZoneIds] to inspect which region identifiers the current platform bundle ships with.
 * - Construct a [FixedOffsetTimeZone] directly when a protocol carries a concrete [UtcOffset].
 *
 * ## Usage example
 * ```

 6→package kotlinx.datetime

 8→import kotlin.time.*
 9→
10→/**
11→ * A source of [Instant] values.
12→ *
13→ * See [Clock.System][Clock.System] for the clock instance that queries the operating system.
14→ */
15→public interface Clock {
16→    /**
17→     * Returns the [Instant] corresponding to the current time, according to this clock.
18→     */
19→    public fun now(): Instant
20→
21→    /**
22→     * The [Clock] instance that queries the operating system as its source of knowledge of time.
23→     */
24→    public object System : Clock {
25→        override fun now(): Instant = @Suppress("DEPRECATION_ERROR") Instant.now()
26→    }
27→
28→    public companion object {
29→
30→    }
31→}
32→
33→/**
34→ * Returns the current date at the given [time zone][timeZone], according to [this Clock][this].
35→ */
36→public fun Clock.todayIn(timeZone: TimeZone): LocalDate =
37→    now().toLocalDateTime(timeZone).date
38→
39→/**
40→ * Returns a [TimeSource] that uses this [Clock] to mark a time instant and to find the amount of time elapsed since that mark.
41→ */
42→@ExperimentalTime
43→public fun Clock.asTimeSource(): TimeSource.WithComparableMarks = object : TimeSource.WithComparableMarks {
44→    override fun markNow(): ComparableTimeMark = InstantTimeMark(now(), this@asTimeSource)
45→}
46→
47→@ExperimentalTime
48→private class InstantTimeMark(private val instant: Instant, private val clock: Clock) : ComparableTimeMark {
49→    override fun elapsedNow(): Duration = saturatingDiff(clock.now(), instant)
50→
51→    override fun plus(duration: Duration): ComparableTimeMark = InstantTimeMark(instant.saturatingAdd(duration), clock)
52→    override fun minus(duration: Duration): ComparableTimeMark = InstantTimeMark(instant.saturatingAdd(-duration), clock)
53→
54→    override fun minus(other: ComparableTimeMark): Duration {
55→        if (other !is InstantTimeMark || other.clock != this.clock) {
56→            throw IllegalArgumentException("Subtracting or comparing time marks from different time sources is not possible: $this and $other")
57→        }
58→        return saturatingDiff(this.instant, other.instant)
59→    }
60→
61→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
62→        return other is InstantTimeMark && this.clock == other.clock && this.instant == other.instant
63→    }
64→
65→    override fun hashCode(): Int = instant.hashCode()
66→
67→    override fun toString(): String = "InstantTimeMark($instant, $clock)"
68→
69→    private fun Instant.isSaturated() = this == Instant.MAX || this == Instant.MIN
70→    private fun Instant.saturatingAdd(duration: Duration): Instant {
71→        if (isSaturated()) {
72→            if (duration.isInfinite() && duration.isPositive() != this.isDistantFuture) {
73→                throw IllegalArgumentException("Summing infinities of different signs")
74→            }
75→            return this
76→        }
77→        return this + duration
78→    }
79→    private fun saturatingDiff(instant1: Instant, instant2: Instant): Duration = when {
80→        instant1 == instant2 ->
81→            Duration.ZERO
82→        instant1.isSaturated() || instant2.isSaturated() ->
83→            (instant1 - instant2) * Double.POSITIVE_INFINITY
84→        else ->
85→            instant1 - instant2
86→    }
87→}
88→
89→@Deprecated("Use Clock.todayIn instead", ReplaceWith("this.todayIn(timeZone)"), DeprecationLevel.WARNING)
90→public fun Clock.todayAt(timeZone: TimeZone): LocalDate = todayIn(timeZone)

16→ * The conversion is lossy: Darwin uses millisecond precision to represent dates, and [Instant] allows for nanosecond
17→ * resolution.

19→public fun Instant.toNSDate(): NSDate {
20→    val secs = epochSeconds * 1.0 + nanosecondsOfSecond / 1.0e9
21→    if (secs < NSDate.distantPast.timeIntervalSince1970 || secs > NSDate.distantFuture.timeIntervalSince1970) {
22→        throw IllegalArgumentException("Boundaries of NSDate exceeded")
23→    }
24→    return NSDate.dateWithTimeIntervalSince1970(secs)
25→}

29→ *
30→ * Even though Darwin only uses millisecond precision, it is possible that [date] uses larger resolution, storing
31→ * microseconds or even nanoseconds. In this case, the sub-millisecond parts of [date] are rounded to the nearest
32→ * millisecond, given that they are likely to be conversion artifacts.
33→ */
34→public fun NSDate.toKotlinInstant(): Instant {
35→    val secs = timeIntervalSince1970()
36→    val millis = secs * 1000 + if (secs > 0) 0.5 else -0.5
37→    return Instant.fromEpochMilliseconds(millis.toLong())
38→}
